rulings.Case Title: MARY ROBERTA HOLLAND VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Verifications To Responses To Set One Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, Requests For Admissions And Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Imo Industries¿ motion to compel plaintiffs to provide verifications is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Jean Ann Mccullery And Evan Lloyd Bennett To Provide Initial Responses To Discovery; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion to compel plaintiffs Jean Ann McCullery and Evan Lloyd Bennett to provide initial responses to discovery is off calendar as moot given plaintiff¿s representation that the complaint will be amended to name Ms. McCullery and Mr. Bennett as nominal defendants. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Pltf Alma Jeannette Bennett To Provide Further Responses To Discovery; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion to compel plaintiff Alma Jeannette Bennett to provide further responses to discovery is granted as to requests for admission nos. 23-101 and taken off calendar as moot as to special interrogatory no. 27 and requests for admission nos. 1, 16-19 and 21. As to requests for admission nos. 23-101, plaintiff¿s responses do not comply with C.C.P. 2033.220(b). Plaintiff has 10 days to serve supplemental responses. Defendant¿s moving papers did not provide notice that it was requesting sanctions so its request for sanctions that was only included in its reply is denied. (See C.C.P. 2023.030, 2023.040.) Defendant represented that the motion is now moot as to special interrogatory no. 27 and requests for admission nos. 1, 16-19 and 21. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Further Deposition Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel further deposition of the person most knowledgeable and custodian of records of defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals and request for sanctions: hearing required. The hearing will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiffs¿ Interrogatories By Kaiser Foundation Hospitals And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel answers to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories by Kaiser Foundation Hospitals is granted. General Order 129 requires that defendant provide such responses. Defendant must provide its responses within 14 days of the date of this hearing. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions in the amount of $1840.00 is granted. Plaintiffs¿ moving papers properly noticed its request for sanctions and defendant failed to demonstrate substantial justification why it has not yet provided its annual updates. Plaintiffs¿ request that hearings on any dispositive motions filed by defendant in asbestos cases before this court be stayed until 60 days after defendant has provided verified responses is granted. Defendant¿s opposition was timely and was considered by the court. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30 am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
